description_long: This object is a stylized flowering plant with broad, flat green leaves radiating outward from a central base. The plant features several pale pink flowers, each with five distinct petals arranged in a star-like pattern, and a few unopened or less prominent buds in a muted brownish-green color. The leaves are lanceolate, with pointed tips and visible angular facets, giving them a geometric appearance. The flowers are positioned above the foliage, supported by slender stems that converge at the base. The overall form is compact and low to the ground, typical of ornamental flowers found in urban parks or landscaped areas. The coloration is soft and pastel, contributing to a gentle, decorative aesthetic suitable for public or residential green spaces.
description: A compact flowering plant with pale pink, five-petaled flowers and broad, angular green leaves radiating from the base. The flowers are star-shaped and elevated above the foliage on slender stems.
description_view_0: Front view showing a prominent central pink flower with five petals, surrounded by broad green leaves and two less distinct flowers in the background.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the side of the main flower and reveals more of the leaf structure, with one flower partially obscured.
description_view_2: Two pink flowers are clearly visible, both with five petals, and the green leaves are symmetrically spread out beneath them.
description_view_3: The view shows the two main flowers from a different angle, emphasizing the overlapping petals and the geometric arrangement of the leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, star-like configuration with each flower displaying five distinct, non-overlapping petals. The arrangement is symmetrical and whorled, typical of many ornamental flowering plants.
